commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-normalizable tool-schema quarantine before normal attempt, compaction, and doctor normalization, plus focused projection/quarantine tests and synthetic fixture names.

PR surface: Source +346, Tests +413. Total +759 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main normal attempt and compaction paths normalize active tool arrays before quarantine, while the PR tests cover unreadable rows/descriptors and unsupported schemas. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow automated repair remains; maintainers need to decide whether to accept the compatibility semantics and merge once checks are satisfactory.

target.title: fix(agents): harden runtime tool schema diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- quarantines active runtime tools whose schema rows/descriptors are unreadable or unsupported before they reach provider/model normalization
- applies the same pre-normalization quarantine to compaction, including core tools and post-policy bundled MCP/LSP tools
- filters unreadable bundled MCP/LSP tool candidates before bundled allowlist/policy handling, then quarantines only post-policy active bundled tools
- adds doctor runtime checks using the same active tool projection path so unsupported active schemas surface before assistant turns
- keeps provider-repairable schemas available for provider normalization, avoids warning on disabled/policy-filtered tools, and uses only synthetic `fuzzplugin` / `mockplugin` fixtures

## Verification

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`: 3 routed shards, 125 tests passed on head `51d36ab5040`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.harness.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.ts src/agents/tool-schema-quarantine.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- changed-file/spec scrub for the reported plugin/tool names and too-close synthetic suffixes: clean
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean on current `origin/main` `3e7f745`
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`: AWS Crabbox run `run_6560a7dbb670`, lease `cbx_9c2b30943f0f`, type `c7a.8xlarge`, lanes `core`, `coreTests`, exit 0 on current `origin/main` `3e7f745`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: active runtime tool schemas that would crash projection before an assistant turn are filtered/quarantined and surfaced through doctor diagnostics instead of poisoning the whole assistant startup path.

Real environment tested: fresh linked OpenClaw `gwt` worktree from `origin/main`, plus AWS Crabbox Linux remote changed gate for the final rebased head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: split the runtime/doctor slice onto current main, handled the compaction sibling-path review finding, scrubbed synthetic test names, ran focused projection/quarantine/doctor/embedded-attempt/compaction tests, formatter check, diff check, changed-file/spec scrub, autoreview, and AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: tests prove unsupported schemas are quarantined before model session creation, unreadable tool rows do not crash explicit allowlists, unreadable bundled tool names do not crash bundled allowlist policy, compaction quarantines malformed active tools before provider normalization, disabled/policy-filtered tools do not produce quarantine telemetry, runtime quarantine logging avoids re-reading bad rows, and doctor reports active runtime schema findings through the same projection shape.

Observed result after fix: a malformed active tool is omitted from the model-facing runtime tool list and reported as a quarantine/doctor finding while healthy sibling tools continue.

What was not tested: no live Telegram/UI round trip for this slice; behavior is covered through embedded-attempt runtime tests, compaction tests, doctor tests, autoreview, and the remote changed gate.
